The '''Rhen Var Citadel''' was an ancient structure on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Rhen Var/Legends|Rhen Var]].<ref name="BFI" /> It included a large courtyard at the base of a four-story tower, an ancient crypt, and a domed observatory. A keep was in the center of the Citadel, and the watch was south of the keep. There were [[Statuary|statues]] in front of the watch and in the keep,<ref name="BFI guide">[[Star Wars Battlefront: Prima Official Game Guide|''Star Wars Battlefront'': Prima Official Game Guide]]</ref> which were made in honor of [[Ross Mysliwiec]], an ancient [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]]. The Citadel would be the site of several battles.

==History==
===The Clone Wars===
During the [[Battle of Rhen Var (Clone Wars)|Battle of Rhen Var]], forces of both the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] entered the citadel to fight when the CIS attempted to use the site as a staging ground for coordinating the landing of reinforcements far away at the site of the [[Jedi monument (Rhen Var)|Jedi monument]], where [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]] was fighting to gain access to the temple in order to find the tomb of [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]]. The droids occupying the Citadel were quickly put down by a detachment of clone troopers, the battle leaving the citadel in ruins.

===The Galactic Civil War===
During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] the Citadel was used by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], which had set up a monitoring station to observe [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|fleet]] movements.<ref name="BFI" /> When the Rebels discovered the Imperial station, they launched an [[Attack on Rhen Var|attack]] to capture it.<ref name="Atlas">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> They took the Citadel but were quickly [[Sabotage at Rhen Var|defeated]] when Imperial forces launched a counterattack a few days later to defend their territory.<ref name="Atlas" />

{{Struct-stub}}

==Behind the scenes==
The Rhen Var Citadel appears in a map in the [[2004]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Battlefront]]''. During the Historical Campaign, the battle on the map appears as an Imperial mission, although the game's story is [[Canon|secondary continuity]], according to [[Leland Chee]], the keeper of [[Lucasfilm]]'s [[Holocron continuity database]].<ref name="Blog">{{Blog|holocron|56|starwars.com forum HolocronKeeper comments archive}}</ref> The map is also available in the game's [[Star Wars: Battlefront II|sequel]] via an [[Wikipedia:Xbox|Xbox]] Live patch. However, prior to the xbox update, this map would have been available on the PSP version. It can be found in the UMD disc of the game and be edited back into it.
